title: Flutter App Development
metaTitle: Flutter App Development Services | Maaz Zindani
metaDescription: Meta-certified Flutter developer building cross-platform iOS and Android apps from one codebase — production-grade, fast, and scaled to 100k+ users.
keywords: ios and android app one codebase
intro: I build cross-platform apps in Flutter — one codebase that ships a native-feeling experience to both iOS and Android. Over six years and 15+ shipped apps, I've taken Flutter products from a blank repo to the stores and up to 100k+ users.

sections.body: A single Flutter codebase delivering polished iOS and Android apps, clean state management (BLoC / Cubit or Riverpod), a real backend, CI/CD, and store submission handled end to end.

One team, one codebase, two platforms — Flutter cuts the cost and time of maintaining separate native apps while keeping 60fps performance and a native look. It's the right default for most product teams shipping to both stores.
Dispatch Pro (logistics super-app, 2,500 drivers in 60 days), Qist Bazaar (BNPL, 50k installs), and Nuclear Home Video (100k+ users at 4.6★) are all Flutter or native builds I shipped and scaled.

faq.q: How long does a Flutter app take to build?
faq.a: A focused MVP is typically around 90 days from idea to app store. Larger platforms scale from there, delivered on a two-week sprint cadence.

faq.q: Can Flutter handle complex, high-scale apps?
faq.a: Yes. I've shipped Flutter apps handling real-time logistics, fintech transactions, and 100k+ users. With the right architecture Flutter scales comfortably.

faq.q: Do you build the backend too?
faq.a: Yes — I deliver end to end: Flutter frontend plus Laravel / Spring Boot / Node backends, AWS infrastructure, and CI/CD pipelines.

title: MVP Development for Startups
metaTitle: Startup MVP Development in 90 Days | Maaz Zindani
metaDescription: Idea to app store in about 90 days — Flutter-first MVPs on production-grade backends, built so you can raise, launch, and scale without a rebuild.
intro: I help founders go from idea to a real, in-store MVP in roughly 90 days — built on foundations solid enough to scale, so your first version doesn't become a throwaway.

sections.body: Discovery and scoping, a Flutter-first build on a production-grade backend, and store launch — delivered in two-week sprints so you see working software every fortnight, not at the end.

sections.h2: Built to scale, not to throw away
sections.body: Too many MVPs get rebuilt the moment they get traction. I architect yours with clean state management, a real backend, and CI/CD from day one, so growth means adding features — not starting over.
Qist Bazaar went from zero to 50k installs and 12M GMV in six months. Dispatch Pro shipped v1 in nine weeks and onboarded thousands of users. That's the MVP-to-scale path in practice.

faq.a: Discovery call within 48 hours, and a kickoff typically inside two weeks.
It depends on scope, but I work in fixed sprints so cost is predictable — we scope the smallest version that proves the idea and build from there.

faq.q: Do you work with non-technical founders?
faq.a: Absolutely. I own the technical side end to end — product architecture, mobile, backend, and release — so you can focus on customers.

title: Mobile App Developer in Karachi
metaTitle: Mobile App Developer in Karachi, Pakistan | Maaz Zindani
metaDescription: Karachi-based, Meta-certified mobile app developer and Flutter expert — building iOS and Android apps for clients in Pakistan and worldwide, async-friendly across timezones.
intro: I'm a Karachi-based, Meta-certified mobile app developer and Flutter expert. I build for clients across Pakistan and worldwide — same craft, timezone-friendly collaboration, and a track record of apps that reach 100k+ users.

sections.body: Based in Karachi, I lead mobile builds for founders and companies far beyond Pakistan — shipping worldwide and working async across timezones. I studied Artificial Intelligence at SZABIST and have spent six years shipping products people actually use.
Cross-platform Flutter and native Android/iOS apps, with the backend and DevOps behind them — logistics, fintech, IoT, e-commerce, and enterprise tools.

sections.h2: Why work with a Karachi developer
sections.body: You get senior, Meta-certified engineering and full end-to-end ownership at a rate that's competitive globally — plus a portfolio of shipped, scaled apps to back it up.

faq.q: Do you work with international clients?
faq.a: Yes — based in Karachi, Pakistan, I ship worldwide and stay async-friendly across timezones.

faq.q: Are you available for on-site work in Karachi?
faq.a: I work primarily remote but am based in Karachi and open to discussing on-site collaboration for local projects.

faq.q: What kinds of apps do you build?
faq.a: Mobile apps across logistics, fintech, IoT, e-commerce, streaming and enterprise — Flutter and native, with the backend and DevOps included.

title: Backend & DevOps
metaDescription: Production backends and DevOps pipelines behind your app — Laravel, Spring Boot and Node on AWS with Docker, Kubernetes and CI/CD for zero-downtime releases.
intro: An app is only as good as what's behind it. I build the production backends and DevOps pipelines that keep mobile products fast, reliable, and cheap to run at scale.

sections.body: Laravel, Spring Boot, and Node.js APIs designed for real traffic — clean data models, safe queries, and resilience against the messy edge cases that only show up in production.
AWS infrastructure with Docker and Kubernetes pipelines, CI/CD via GitHub Actions and fastlane, and release cadences measured in days — with zero downtime and a 99.9% uptime line held in production.
Dispatch Pro runs on a Spring Boot + Kafka pipeline on AWS with releases every few days. QistLock is a NestJS SaaS running live. I've kept fleets of apps at 99.9% uptime.

faq.q: Can you take over an existing backend?
faq.a: Yes — I regularly inherit codebases, stabilise them, and add CI/CD and monitoring so releases stop being scary.
Primarily AWS, with Docker and Kubernetes. I also work with Firebase and self-hosted VPS setups where they fit the budget better.
Yes — GitHub Actions, fastlane, and containerised pipelines for zero-downtime deploys are a standard part of what I deliver.

title: AI Integration for Apps
metaTitle: AI Integration for Mobile Apps | Maaz Zindani
metaDescription: Add real AI to your product — LLM features, RAG pipelines and automation embedded into mobile and web apps by a developer who ships production software.
intro: I integrate AI into products in ways that actually ship — LLM features, retrieval-augmented generation, and automation embedded into real mobile and web apps, not demos.

sections.body: LLM-powered features (chat, summarisation, extraction), RAG pipelines that ground answers in your own data, and automation that removes manual steps — wired into your app with the reliability and cost control production demands.

AI features fail when they hallucinate or cost too much. I design retrieval, guardrails, and caching so the AI is accurate, fast, and affordable at scale — with a fallback path when the model is uncertain.
I hold Google AI Essentials and IBM's Exploratory Data Analysis for Machine Learning, and I studied Artificial Intelligence at university — so the AI work rests on real fundamentals.

faq.q: Which AI models do you work with?
faq.a: I default to the latest and most capable models for the task, and design the integration so you can swap providers without a rewrite.

faq.q: Can you add AI to my existing app?
faq.a: Yes — AI features can be layered onto an existing Flutter or web app, from a chat assistant to a RAG-powered search over your content.

faq.q: How do you keep AI costs under control?
faq.a: With caching, retrieval to keep prompts small, and model selection tuned per task — so quality stays high without a runaway bill.

title: App Modernization & Migration
metaTitle: App Modernization & Migration to Flutter | Maaz Zindani
metaDescription: Migrate legacy native apps to a unified Flutter architecture with zero downtime — one modern codebase, faster releases, and lower maintenance cost.
intro: If you're maintaining two aging native apps — or one that's grown brittle — I migrate them to a single, modern Flutter architecture, without stopping your releases.

sections.body: Separate iOS and Android codebases mean double the work and drift between platforms. I consolidate them into one Flutter codebase with clean architecture, so features ship once and behave the same everywhere.
Migrations are done incrementally — feature by feature, with the old and new coexisting where needed — so users never see a broken release and the business keeps shipping throughout.

I've repaired and modernised large codebases — including a 235-file client with hundreds of broken imports brought back to zero unresolved — and kept fleets of apps at 99.9% uptime through the transition.

faq.q: Do we have to stop releasing during a migration?
faq.a: No. I migrate incrementally so you keep shipping. The old and new can run side by side until the switch is safe.
One codebase instead of two means faster features, consistent behaviour across platforms, and materially lower maintenance cost.

faq.q: Can you rescue a stalled or broken codebase?
faq.a: Yes — stabilising inherited, broken, or half-finished codebases and getting them shipping again is a large part of what I do.
